questions.question: Babur was the founder of the great __________ Empire of India.
Babur ascended the throne as ruler of __________, a small principality in Transoxiana.
Because of the lineage of Timur, the Mughals were also referred to as __________.
__________ was the Governor of Punjab who invited Babur to invade India.
Babur used artillery and __________ for the first time in India.
The defeat of Rana Sanga at __________ gave Babur complete control over Central India.
Babur's autobiography __________ is a masterpiece of literature.
Babur introduced __________ palaces in India.
Humayun's three brothers were Kamran, __________ and Hindal.
Humayun was defeated by Sher Shah in the Battle of __________ in 1539.
After losing the Battle of Kannauj, Humayun became an exile for the next __________ years.
Humayun recaptured Kabul and Kandahar with the help of the ruler of __________.
Humayun died in 1556 due to a fall from the staircase of his __________.
Sher Shah's real name was __________.
Sher Shah founded the __________ dynasty.
Sher Shah died in 1545 while besieging the fort of __________.
Sher Shah divided his empire into 47 units called __________.
Each sarkar was subdivided into __________.
The system of branding horses with an imperial sign was called __________.
The state's share of produce under Sher Shah was __________ of the produce.
The amount of revenue was recorded in a paper called __________.
The Grand Trunk Road ran from __________ in Bengal to Peshawar.
Sher Shah's greatest contribution was the introduction of the silver __________.
Gold coins introduced by Sher Shah were called __________ and weighed 169 grams.
__________ authored the Humayun Nama, which described the everyday life of the royal family.
